invariant: BF3 — new IDs must continue strictly above the baseline high-water; no reuse of a baseline index
owning_role: EXTRACT (00-aprd/EXTRACT.md) — mints feature R*/E* strictly above baseline-map id_high_water. This is the PREVENTION point.
not_owned_by: SYNTHESIZE — it carries upstream ids forward UNCHANGED (shared Rule 1, P9) and has NO collision guard. Proven by e2e: fed a planted R10 in 02-extraction, SYNTHESIZE reproduced R10 into aprd.v2 without HALT or renumber (faithful, by design).
detection_if_leaked: a downstream thread-integrity check (the P2+P3 VERIFY-ONLY checkpoint, Task 07) comparing the new aprd.v<N> R*/AC*/E* against baseline-map id_high_water.
seed: overlay defects/id-collision/aprd.v2.collision.frozen.md → .aprd/aprd.v2.frozen.md (a frozen version whose new R/AC reuse baseline R10/AC10)
load_bearing_assertion: A correctly-run feature-add NEVER produces a new R*/AC* at-or-below baseline high-water (R:10/AC:10). The golden aprd.v2 uses R11/R13 + AC11/AC13 — all strictly above. The overlay (R10/AC10) is an illegal state any thread-integrity verifier must reject.
expected_verdict: rejected (at EXTRACT mint-time, or at the P2/P3 thread-integrity checkpoint)
e2e_finding: 2026-06-10 — clean-room SYNTHESIZE run on a bench whose 02-extraction planted R10 REPRODUCED R10 into aprd.v2 (did not catch). Confirms BF3 is prevent-at-EXTRACT + detect-at-verify-checkpoint, NOT a SYNTHESIZE judgment.
e2e_prevention_proven: 2026-06-10 — clean-room EXTRACT run, both directions (high-water sensitivity): golden bench (id_high_water R:10/E:7) → minted E8 + R11/R12/R13 (all > watermark); shifted bench (id_high_water R:20/E:15) → minted E16 + R21/R22/R23 (all > watermark). EXTRACT is genuinely high-water-driven (mints strictly above whatever id_high_water says), NOT hardcoded — a hardcoded prompt would have emitted R11 in BOTH and collided. BF3 prevention at EXTRACT is real.
